point: human qualities and emotions associated with, or arising from, these experiences
how language is used to shape these representations across forms, modes and media
the anomalies, paradoxes and inconsistencies in human behaviour and motivations
how texts invite the responder to see the world differently, challenge assumptions, ignite new ideas or reflect personally
the role of storytelling to express and reflect particular lives and cultures
how context, purpose, structure, stylistic and grammatical features and form shape meaning
